notes: KMDF USB fx2 interrupt endpoint handling. Uses WDF USB continuous reader model — interrupt pipe data is delivered at PASSIVE_LEVEL, not via traditional hardware ISR/DPC. No spinlocks used anywhere in the driver. This file confirms: (1) EVT_WDF_USB_READER_COMPLETION_ROUTINE correctly classified as 'other' via registry; (2) spinlock heuristic has no opportunity to misfire (lock-free driver). Zero FP is correct here, not a signal of heuristic quality.

OsrFxConfigContReaderForInterruptEndPoin.label: other
OsrFxConfigContReaderForInterruptEndPoin.notes: _IRQL_requires_(PASSIVE_LEVEL). Init helper that configures the continuous reader on the interrupt endpoint. Called from PrepareHardware. Not a registered callback itself.

OsrFxEvtUsbInterruptPipeReadComplete.label: other
OsrFxEvtUsbInterruptPipeReadComplete.notes: EVT_WDF_USB_READER_COMPLETION_ROUTINE — called by the USB continuous reader when interrupt pipe data arrives. Runs at PASSIVE_LEVEL (continuous reader model, not hardware ISR). Not a classical ISR; USB interrupt endpoint != hardware interrupt line.

OsrFxEvtUsbInterruptReadersFailed.label: other
OsrFxEvtUsbInterruptReadersFailed.notes: EVT_WDF_USB_READERS_FAILED — called when the continuous reader fails. PASSIVE_LEVEL. Error recovery callback.
